process_name: Charged rare kaon decay K+ -> pi+ nu nubar
implementation_difficulty_reason: Requires a new Delta F = 1 semileptonic s -> d nu nubar operator basis and branching-ratio formula with SM--NP interference, but uses standard clean hadronic input.

required_grep_commands.command: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd scripts tests docs -g '!*.ipynb'
required_grep_commands.result_summary: Only existing Delta F = 2 neutral-meson and mu->e gamma surfaces; no K+ -> pi+ nu nubar implementation.

required_grep_commands.command: rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd scripts tests docs -g '!*.ipynb'
required_grep_commands.result_summary: Hits are catalog plan text, generic neutrino utilities, and unrelated flavor surfaces; no live charged rare-kaon branching-ratio constraint.

required_grep_commands.command: rg -n "MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|evaluate_d0_mixing|check_mu_to_e_gamma" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams tests docs -g '!*.ipynb'
required_grep_commands.result_summary: Modern and legacy quark surfaces enumerate epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0 Delta F = 2 systems only.

required_grep_commands.command: rg -n "K\^?\+.*pi\^?\+.*nu|K\+.*pi\+.*nu|pi\+.*nu.*nu|pi.*nu.*nubar|Kp.*Pip|K.*pi.*nubar|NA62|KOTO" quarkConstraints flavorConstraints scanParams neutrinos yukawa qcd scripts tests docs -g '!*.ipynb'
required_grep_commands.result_summary: Specific rare-kaon search returns only catalog planning documents, not implementation files.

near_miss_evidence: quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 enumerates only epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, D0.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:211 defines epsilon_K, not K+ -> pi+ nu nubar.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:755 defines Delta m_K helper, not a Delta F = 1 semileptonic decay.

sections.pdg_prose:
The YAML sidecar uses the latest NA62 experimental-equivalent combination,
rather than an older PDG average:
\[
  \BRof{K^+ \to \pi^+ \nu\bar{\nu}}
  = \left(9.6^{+1.9}_{-1.8}\right)\times 10^{-11},
\]
from NA62 data collected in 2016--2024, traced to
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/K004/na62\_2026\_arxiv2604\_12649.txt}.
The published 2016--2022 observation anchor is
\(\left(13.0^{+3.3}_{-3.0}\right)\times 10^{-11}\), with 51 signal candidates
and an expected background of \(18^{+3}_{-2}\) events; see
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/K004/na62\_2025\_arxiv2412\_12015.txt}.
For SM comparison, the sidecar records the Buras--Venturini benchmark
\(\BR_{\rm SM}=\left(8.60\pm0.42\right)\times10^{-11}\) and the
Brod--Gorbahn--Stamou update
\(\BR_{\rm SM}=7.73(61)\times10^{-11}\).
sections.relevance:
Anarchic warped models can generate misaligned \(s\)-\(d\) neutral currents
through KK gauge exchange, \(Z\)-coupling shifts, or related electroweak
matching effects.  Unlike the current quark lane's \(\DeltaFtwo\) kaon
observables, this is a \(\Delta F=1\) semileptonic process.  It therefore
tests whether the model has controlled short-distance neutral currents beyond
the four-quark \(K\)-mixing sector.  The charged mode is especially useful
because it is theoretically clean, uses a hadronic matrix element tied to
semileptonic kaon data, and can interfere with the SM amplitude rather than
only setting an incoherent upper bound.
sections.post_2008:
The CFW-era RS discussion used \(\DeltaFtwo\) flavor bounds as the central
pressure point, quoting a generic RS KK-gluon mass scale of about \(21\) TeV
and a composite pseudo-Goldstone scenario scale of about \(33\) TeV.  Since
that era, NA62 reported the published observation in 2025 and a newer 2026
preliminary combination with better-than-\(20\%\) precision.  On the theory
side, post-2008 SM updates have sharpened the short-distance prediction and
exposed the role of
CKM-input choices: Brod--Gorbahn--Stamou quote \(7.73(61)\times10^{-11}\),
while Buras--Venturini quote \((8.60\pm0.42)\times10^{-11}\) using a strategy
designed to avoid the direct \(|V_{cb}|\) and \(|V_{ub}|\) tensions.
sections.validity:
As an experimental branching-ratio constraint this mode is clean, but its use
inside an RS scan is model-dependent.  A production constraint needs Wilson
coefficients for
\((\bar{s}\gamma_\mu P_{L,R}d)(\bar{\nu}\gamma^\mu(1-\gamma_5)\nu)\), a
choice of neutrino-flavor treatment, and a convention for SM--NP interference.
The Buras--Venturini SM number also assumes no new-physics infection in the
\(\DeltaFtwo\) inputs used to remove CKM ambiguities, so a checker should keep
that assumption visible if the catalog later turns this process into a hard
bound.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  The required grep sweep finds kaon mixing, generic
neutrino-mass utilities, and the catalog planning row, but no implementation
of \(K^+\to\pi^+\nu\bar{\nu}\), no \(\Delta F=1\) \(s\to d\nu\bar{\nu}\)
operator, and no branching-ratio acceptance gate.  The closest quark-code
surfaces are the \(\DeltaFtwo\) entries in
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py} and the modern policy list
\texttt{MODERN\_PHENOMENOLOGY\_SYSTEM\_IDS = (epsilon\_K, K, B\_d, B\_s, D0)}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{MEDIUM.}  The observable needs a new \(\Delta F=1\)
semileptonic operator basis and a branching-ratio formula with SM--NP
interference, but the hadronic input is standard and this channel does not
require the angular likelihood machinery needed for many \(b\to s\ell\ell\)
observables.  The difficulty would become HIGH only if the catalog requires a
full RS tower and neutrino-sector matching rather than an EFT Wilson input.
sections.key_references:
Process-local reference keys before bibliography consolidation:
NA62:KpPipNunu2026, NA62:KpPipNunu2025,
BurasVenturini:KpPipNunu2022, BrodGorbahnStamou:KpPipNunu2021, and
CsakiFalkowskiWeiler:RSFlavor2008.

anchors.anchor_string: CFW abstract quotes a generic RS KK-gluon mass bound of about 21 TeV and about 33 TeV for the composite pseudo-Goldstone scenario

priority_rationale_short: Clean K+ -> pi+ nu nubar constraint with 20% data
priority_rationale_full:
K+ -> pi+ nu nubar is a clean short-distance Delta S=1 probe of flavor-changing
Z, Z', and related electroweak neutral-current effects in RS models. The
current NA62-era measurement is close to the Standard Model with roughly
20 percent precision, so it can meaningfully limit large semileptonic
amplitudes. It remains subleading to Delta F=2 kaon mixing in canonical
anarchic scans.
